name: Multi-Actor Lab Protocol with Lifelines and Parallel Processing
input: Create a sequence diagram for a multi-site biobanking protocol. Actors: Clinician, Biobank Coordinator, Sample Processing Lab, Quality Control, LIMS Database. Flow: Clinician collects sample and sends to Biobank Coordinator. Coordinator logs receipt in LIMS Database. Coordinator sends sample to Sample Processing Lab. Sample Processing Lab activates and processes in parallel: aliquoting, DNA extraction, and plasma separation. After processing, Lab deactivates and sends results to Quality Control. QC runs integrity checks. If QC passes, QC updates LIMS Database with sample status and notifies Coordinator of availability. If QC fails, QC sends sample back to Processing Lab for reprocessing (loop up to 3 times). After all processing, Coordinator notifies Clinician of sample availability. Include activate/deactivate on Processing Lab and QC lifelines.
qualityCriteria: All 5 participants declared (Clinician, Coordinator, Lab, QC, LIMS)
Activate/deactivate used on at least one participant
Parallel block (par) for simultaneous sample processing
At least 3 parallel processing steps mentioned (aliquoting, DNA extraction, plasma)
Alt or opt block for QC pass/fail branching
Loop construct for reprocessing (up to 3 times)
LIMS Database interactions present (log and update)
Valid Mermaid syntax that parses without errors
napkinBenchmark: Sequence diagram with 5 actors, activate/deactivate lifelines, par block for 3 parallel processes, alt for QC, loop for reprocessing.
